meaning: n.喧嚣,喧哗,吵闹(声),(混乱,骚动)声
examples: I was scared by the hullabaloo over my arrival.
There are many people who "the moment they alight from the official carriage" make a hullabaloo.
There was a hullabaloo about it.
But the coming hullabaloo over Ryan's budget proposals may distract the economically pained from punishing Obama.
The single will be backed by a previously "lost" track, Mobile Alabama School Leaving Hullabaloo.
